Tests the non-associative property of string and number concatenation in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the associativity of string and number concatenation in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression `("1" + 1) + 1` does not equal `"1" + (1 + 1)`. The test demonstrates how JavaScript handles type coercion and operator precedence when combining strings and numbers. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the JavaScript language.
